steps.title: Identify the appositive phrase and its reference
steps.content: The opening phrase “A recipient of the Othmer Gold Medal and the Davy Medal from the Royal Society,” is an appositive that describes a person — the award recipient.

steps.title: Apply the appositive rule to the choices
steps.content:
An appositive must be immediately followed by the noun phrase it describes.

The appositive refers to a medal recipient, so the subject must be that person.

Choice D correctly provides “chemist Ahmed Zewail.” Choices A, B, and C use non-person nouns (results, introductions, limits), which violate the requirement that an appositive precede the noun it names.

That matches choice D — D is correct.
